title: ferray-linalg: norm P(p) for matrices silently falls back to Frobenius
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

matrix_norm() in norms.rs has NormOrder::P(_) which silently computes the Frobenius norm instead of the actual p-norm. NumPy raises a ValueError for invalid matrix norm orders. The comment says '// General p-norm not standard for matrices; use Frobenius'. But NumPy does support matrix norms for p=1, p=2, p=inf, p=-1, p=-2, p=-inf — these are the standard induced norms. The P(p) case should either: (1) raise an error for unsupported p values, or (2) support the standard induced norms p=1,2,inf.
